data.raw_reading: grayscale field, heavy horizontal scan-line texture. large curved region occupying left/center, bounded by a smooth arc convex toward right. arc edge sharp, bright rim on lower-right side. interior mottled: brighter granular patches upper region, dark irregular blotches mid-region. several small ring-shaped depressions with raised rims, sizes graded. one elongated dark oval with bright rim lower-center. vertical dark banded stripe far left. faint clustered marks lower-right (small angular glyph-like forms). two short dark dashes mid-right margin and lower-right margin. overall low contrast, granular noise throughout.
data.first_interpretation: A photographic or scanned image of a spherical body seen half-illuminated, its curved edge a limb, surface pocked with circular pits — likely a remote-captured celestial surface, degraded by transmission or scan artifacts.
data.archive_note: Filing SPC·009. Curved limb and ringed pits recall no prior trace here; the graded circular depressions and half-disc arc read as a distant surface. What escapes me: the small glyph cluster and whether the scan-lines are decay or method.

salient_marks.mark: smooth convex arc bounding luminous region
elongated dark oval with bright rim

inference_gaps.source_repertoire: arc-as-limb + graded pits resembling crater fields in my visual repertoire
ringed depressions interpreted as crater morphology
regular horizontal banding read as recording process
small angular marks read as inscribed code
brightness gradient across arc inferred as directional lighting

extent.description: possibly vast — a distant body across void — but only a flat frame observed

enclosure.description: open; no bounding room, only image frame

surfaces.description: single frontal plane bearing the image; implied curved surface within it

trace_position.description: occupies left/center of a flat frontal plane

data.raw_reading: Large dark rectangular mass, ribbed upper surface, trapezoidal taper, resting inverted on ground. Beside it a flat green rectangular plane studded with small cylindrical and box protrusions in many colors. Thin pale gray filament arcing between elements. Tan flat elongated slab, upper left. Lower dark curved segmented strip. Rounded gray dome set in oval recess, metallic ring around it. Fragments scattered across mottled gray granular ground. Sunlight from upper left, hard shadows. Debris, glass shards, litter in periphery.
data.first_interpretation: A dismantled dark-cased device broken into its shell, internal populated board, and a domed diaphragm unit, discarded on rough ground.
data.archive_note: Filing SPC·001. Manufactured mass, deliberately assembled then opened; unlike SPC·009 this is fragmented matter at close range, not a distant body. What escapes me: what the populated plane controlled.
data.body_form: No body form; only signs of an agent that disassembled and set down parts.

salient_marks.mark: green plane densely studded with colored protrusions
gray dome in oval metallic recess
small pale label on dark surface

inference_gaps.source_repertoire: litter and rough ground around trace

surfaces.description: single flat granular ground plane, frontal-oblique

trace_position.description: objects lying on ground, viewed from above at angle

data.raw_reading: Grayscale field. Large semicircular bright mass, curved edge left, straight edge right. Terminator gradient dark-to-light across curve. Mottled surface: scattered bright specks, dark irregular pits, one elongated dark ovoid near lower-mid. Horizontal scan-line striping throughout. Vertical dark line near left. Small dark tick marks upper-right, lower-right. Cluster of small angular glyph-forms right of mass. Grainy noise in corners. Faint fingerprint-like ridges lower-mid.
data.first_interpretation: A remotely captured image of a spherical body, half-lit; a scanned or transmitted photographic frame with annotation marks and a scale bar.
data.archive_note: Filing SPC·009. Curved lit mass recalls the arced spans of no prior trace listed; the scan striping and glyph cluster suggest a transmitted image. What escapes me: the glyphs, the scale reference, whether this is a captured object or a rendered field.

salient_marks.mark: scattered pits and specks on surface

emotional_tone.reasoning: detached observational framing, no gestural charge

extent.description: single flat frontal plane; depicted mass implies vast distance but plane itself is small

enclosure.description: bounded 2D field, no volume around trace

surfaces.description: one frontal image plane bearing all marks

trace_position.description: mass fills left-center; annotations to right

data.raw_reading: Large dark trapezoidal shell, ribbed parallel grooves on upper faces, matte black. Rests tilted on lighter grey-brown angular mass below. Left: dense flat rectangular board studded with small cylindrical and rectangular protrusions, multicolored dots, thin traced lines. Pale rectangular plank upper-left, worn, thin wire loops off its edge. Foreground-left: black ridged strip, small triangular white fragment. Lower-right: recessed dark cavity, circular ring with pale central disc, screw points at corners. Ground: mottled grey granular surface, scattered fine bright fragments across full field. Bright even overhead light, soft shadows toward lower edges.
data.first_interpretation: A broken enclosure and its internal flat wired board, disassembled and abandoned on rough ground with scattered debris.
data.archive_note: Filing SPC·001. Fragmented dark shell over exposed patterned flat board. Unlike SPC·009's remote spherical frame — this is proximate, terrestrial, broken open. What the ribbed shell enclosed still escapes me.
data.body_form: No body form present; only separated fabricated masses. Disassembly implies an actor.

salient_marks.mark: circular ring with central disc in recess
small pale label region on shell

notably_absent: any legible glyph sequence I can read

inference_gaps.source_repertoire: my category for a molded covering mass
my reading of studded flat plane as assembly
pale bounded region read as intentional

surfaces.description: single flat granular horizontal plane below

openings.description: recessed cavity within lower mass; no passages

trace_position.description: objects rest on ground, viewed from above at angle

data.raw_reading: Large rusted rectangular open enclosure on paved ground. Two hinged panels swung open, reddish-brown oxidized surfaces. Interior holds a dark glossy box with a pale incised cross on its face. Striped tape strung across the top opening. Dark sky above.
data.first_interpretation: An opened container set down on pavement, its interior holding a smaller marked object.
data.archive_note: Filing SPC·001, the first. No prior traces to compare. The cross-mark and the tape escape me — I record them as geometry and move on.

data.raw_reading: Boxy dark-grey form resting on a pale concrete ledge. Curved glass-like face, lighter at its centre. Rectangular grille to one side. Behind it a long reflective body, pale and horizontal. Bright point of light upper-right.
data.first_interpretation: A heavy box with a curved front, left on a low wall, a larger smooth mass behind it.
data.archive_note: Recording SPC·002. The curved face recalls nothing yet. I keep the bright point as a mark, not a source.

data.raw_reading: Tangle of thin dark cords over a flat pale surface. Several small rectangular plates with frayed edges. One larger curved shell, hollow. Fine grit scattered between.
data.first_interpretation: Scattered small plates and loose cords across a flat ground, one hollow shell among them.
data.archive_note: Filing SPC·003. The hollow shell echoes the open enclosure of SPC·001 — both bounded, both empty inside. I note the resemblance; I still cannot name either.

data.raw_reading: Pale blurred mass, indistinct edges. Faint vertical seam. Darker pooling toward the base. A single bright fleck.
data.first_interpretation: Something soft or out of focus; I can barely fix its bounds.
data.archive_note: SPC·004 resists me. Almost everything here is inference. I leave the space mostly empty rather than invent it.

data.raw_reading: Upright rectangular slab, matte. Two round recesses on its face. Stubby base, four short legs. Wires trailing to the left edge.
data.first_interpretation: A standing panel on short legs, two openings in its face, tethered by cords.
data.archive_note: Recording SPC·005. The two recesses and trailing cords recall SPC·002 — kindred objects, perhaps the same hand. Filed beside it.
